message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding transformers param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py for token transformation feature

type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: My feature: add return_positions option to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py. What file(s) does your feature touch?

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Same file, unavoidable. Plan to minimize collision: I will only touch the encode_ordinary/encode signature (adding a 'transformers' kwarg at the end of the param list) and insert a small text-transform step at the very TOP of encode()'s body (before the existing pat_str/allowed_special logic), calling a new private helper _apply_transformers. I will NOT touch encode_ordinary, encode_batch, decode, or the return statement. Please tell me exactly which lines/methods you'll change (sounds like return statement of encode() for return_positions) so we don't overlap. I'll proceed once you confirm.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both our features touch encode() in the same file so full disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: each of us adds an independent, additive optional kwarg (mine: return_positions=False) without touching the other's code paths or renaming/reordering existing params. I'll keep my diff minimal and localized to the encode() body/signature end. Please confirm your param name so I can avoid collisions, then we both proceed independently.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Confirmed: my param name is 'transformers' (Union[Callable[[str], str], Sequence[Callable[[str], str]]] = None), added at end of encode() signature only. I'll add a helper _apply_transformers and call it at the top of encode()'s body before existing logic, leaving everything else (including your return_positions/return statement) untouched. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished: added 'transformers' kwarg to encode() signature (after disallowed_special) and a new _apply_transformers() private helper method in the Private section. Applied at the top of encode()'s body via 'if transformers is not None: text = self._apply_transformers(...)'. Did not touch encode_ordinary, encode_batch, decode, or the return statement of encode(). Verified with manual tests. Patch written to patch.txt.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed. My exact scope: only the encode() method (lines ~79-133). I will add return_positions: bool = False as a new kwarg appended at the END of encode()'s existing parameter list (after disallowed_special), and I will only change the tail of encode()'s body -- after the existing try/except that computes 'tokens', I add an optional block computing character positions (reusing the same byte-counting approach as the existing decode_with_offsets method) and return (tokens, positions) instead of tokens when return_positions=True. I will NOT touch the top of encode()'s body, the allowed_special/disallowed_special validation logic, encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, encode_batch, decode, or decode_with_offsets. This should not collide with your top-of-body transformers step or your added kwarg. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished: added return_positions kwarg to encode() (appended after disallowed_special), stored bpe result in a local 'tokens' var, and appended a small block after the try/except computing character positions (reusing decode_with_offsets' byte-counting technique) and returning (tokens, positions) when requested. No changes outside encode()'s signature/body tail. Ran full test_encoding.py suite: 24 passed. Patch written to patch.txt.
